findings.message: Command file has no YAML frontmatter block; both `name` and `description` fields are absent, preventing proper slash command registration and agent auto-selection.
Command declares no `allowed-tools` list in frontmatter; tools used at runtime are undeclared, reducing transparency and sandboxability.
Command file has no YAML frontmatter block; both `name` and `description` fields are absent, preventing proper slash command registration and agent auto-selection.
Command declares no `allowed-tools` list in frontmatter; tools used at runtime (Bash, Write for changelog) are undeclared.
Command file has no YAML frontmatter block; both `name` and `description` fields are absent, preventing proper slash command registration and agent auto-selection.
Command declares no `allowed-tools` list in frontmatter; tools used at runtime (Bash, Write for changelog) are undeclared.
Command file has no YAML frontmatter block; both `name` and `description` fields are absent, preventing proper slash command registration and agent auto-selection.

`release.md`, `release-local.md`, and `release-nightly.md` instruct the agent to update `docs-site/content/docs/changelog.mdx`, but `release/SKILL.md` step 4 and `CLAUDE.md` explicitly state this file must not be edited because the changelog page renders directly from `CHANGELOG.md`.
Command declares no `allowed-tools` list in frontmatter; tools used at runtime are undeclared.
"Link to issues/PRs if relevant" uses the vague qualifier `relevant` without defining what relevance means in this context.
Command file has no YAML frontmatter block; both `name` and `description` fields are absent, preventing proper slash command registration and agent auto-selection.
Command declares no `allowed-tools` list in frontmatter; tools used at runtime (Bash for git commands) are undeclared.
"Schedule minimal UI/model mutation with DispatchQueue.main.async only when needed" uses `minimal` without defining what qualifies as minimal mutation.
Root-level `"vercel": "^50.9.5"` is an unpinned semver range.

SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY is passed as a positional CLI argument to `swift scripts/derive_sparkle_public_key.swift "$SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY"`, exposing the private key value in the system process table for the duration of the swift invocation.

A `python3 -c` block interpolates shell variables `$SIGNATURE`, `$DMG_LENGTH`, and `$generated_appcast_path` directly into Python source code, matching the eval-with-variables pattern. Inputs are an EdDSA base64 signature, a stat-derived integer, and a controlled temp path — none can carry a Python injection payload.

The Sparkle signing toolchain is cloned from GitHub (`git clone --depth 1 --branch $SPARKLE_VERSION`) and built from source at release time. Git tags are mutable; a rewritten or hijacked tag would silently substitute malicious build tooling into the release signing pipeline without any integrity check.
"cmux supports wait patterns similar to agent-browser" uses `similar` without specifying which patterns are equivalent and which differ.

The `## Script` section lists the script as `scripts/debug_windows_snapshot.sh` but the workflow step and all examples use the full repo-relative path `skills/cmux-debug-windows/scripts/debug_windows_snapshot.sh`; an agent reading only the Script section will attempt to run the script from the wrong location.
"the panel attempts automatic reconnection within its short retry window" uses `short` without specifying the retry duration or count.
"Include a concise changelog summary in the PR body" uses `concise` without specifying a target length or format.
All npm/bun dependencies use `^` semver ranges, allowing minor and patch version drift on each install and enabling silent supply-chain substitution across the dependency tree.

The `## Script` section lists the script as `scripts/debug_windows_snapshot.sh` but the workflow step and all examples use the full repo-relative path `skills/cmux-debug-windows/scripts/debug_windows_snapshot.sh`; an agent reading only the Script section will attempt to run the script from the wrong location.

`release.md`, `release-local.md`, and `release-nightly.md` instruct the agent to update `docs-site/content/docs/changelog.mdx`, but `release/SKILL.md` step 4 and `CLAUDE.md` explicitly state this file must not be edited because the changelog page renders directly from `CHANGELOG.md`.

"Link to issues/PRs if relevant" uses the vague qualifier `relevant` without defining what relevance means in this context.
"Include a concise changelog summary in the PR body" uses `concise` without specifying a target length or format.
"cmux supports wait patterns similar to agent-browser" uses `similar` without specifying which patterns are equivalent and which differ.
"the panel attempts automatic reconnection within its short retry window" uses `short` without specifying the retry duration or count.
"Schedule minimal UI/model mutation with DispatchQueue.main.async only when needed" uses `minimal` without defining what qualifies as minimal mutation.

A `python3 -c` block interpolates shell variables `$SIGNATURE`, `$DMG_LENGTH`, and `$generated_appcast_path` directly into Python source code, matching the eval-with-variables pattern. Inputs are an EdDSA base64 signature, a stat-derived integer, and a controlled temp path — none can carry a Python injection payload.

SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY is passed as a positional CLI argument to `swift scripts/derive_sparkle_public_key.swift "$SPARKLE_PRIVATE_KEY"`, exposing the private key value in the system process table for the duration of the swift invocation.

The Sparkle signing toolchain is cloned from GitHub (`git clone --depth 1 --branch $SPARKLE_VERSION`) and built from source at release time. Git tags are mutable; a rewritten or hijacked tag would silently substitute malicious build tooling into the release signing pipeline without any integrity check.
All npm/bun dependencies use `^` semver ranges, allowing minor and patch version drift on each install and enabling silent supply-chain substitution across the dependency tree.
Root-level `"vercel": "^50.9.5"` is an unpinned semver range.
